behaviorsAudited: createBridgeTool('Bash') returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage delegates to the upstream Bash output component (UserBashOutputMessage or its equivalent) when given a Bash tool result content shape

The toolResult content shape passed to the upstream component carries stdout, stderr, and exit code separately — not joined into one string — so the upstream renderer can color stderr distinctly and surface non-zero exit codes
For non-Bash tool names the bridge tool keeps the existing generic <Text> rendering (no regression for unrouted tools)
message-adapter.ts formatStructuredToolResult for exec_command_end produces a content shape that the bridge Bash tool's renderToolResultMessage can consume

edgeCasesAudited: Bash result with empty stdout AND empty stderr renders the exit-code line and a no-output indicator without crashing
Bash result with non-zero exit code is visually distinct from zero exit code in the rendered output
Bash result with stderr but empty stdout still shows the stderr block
Bash result with stdout containing ANSI escape sequences renders without re-interpreting them as terminal control
Bash result whose stdout exceeds the runtime maxResultSizeChars is truncated rather than crashing the renderer
renderToolResultMessage receives the legacy single-string content (from a tool that did not go through formatStructuredToolResult) and falls back to the generic Text renderer instead of throwing

behaviors.behavior: createBridgeTool('Bash') returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage delegates to the upstream Bash output component (UserBashOutputMessage or its equivalent) when given a Bash tool result content shape
behaviors.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:172-198 (renderToolResultMessage routes via pickToolResultDispatch to BashOutputView). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:23-31 (`expect((node as { type: unknown }).type).toBe(BashOutputView)`) and again with the structured-content-blocks shape at lines 43-57. The vi.mock of `../agenc/upstream/ink.js` (lines 10-18) correctly intercepts the same module path that tool-stubs.tsx imports (`../../agenc/upstream/ink.js` resolves to the same absolute file), so the dispatch path is genuinely exercised end-to-end.

behaviors.behavior: The toolResult content shape passed to the upstream component carries stdout, stderr, and exit code separately — not joined into one string — so the upstream renderer can color stderr distinctly and surface non-zero exit codes
behaviors.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/message-adapter.ts:316-340 (formatStructuredToolResult emits separate {type:'text'} blocks for `<bash-stdout>`, `<bash-stderr>`, and `[exit_code=…]`). Asserted at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.test.ts:186-197 with explicit length=3 and per-block text equality, and at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:236-269 with the wire-shape-lock test that verifies the same blocks survive into BashOutputView's rendered children.

behaviors.behavior: For non-Bash tool names the bridge tool keeps the existing generic <Text> rendering (no regression for unrouted tools)
behaviors.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:194-198 (generic `<Box flexDirection="column"><Text>{joined}</Text></Box>` fallback). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:69-77 (`expect((node as { type: unknown }).type).not.toBe(BashOutputView)` for `createBridgeTool('XYZUnknown')`).

behaviors.behavior: message-adapter.ts formatStructuredToolResult for exec_command_end produces a content shape that the bridge Bash tool's renderToolResultMessage can consume
behaviors.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/message-adapter.ts:316-340. Asserted as a wire-shape lock at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:236-269, which feeds formatStructuredToolResult's output into BashOutputView and asserts both that the joined text contains the upstream tags and that the rendered Text children include the extracted stdout ("out") and stderr ("err").

edgeCases.edgeCase: Bash result with empty stdout AND empty stderr renders the exit-code line and a no-output indicator without crashing
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:80, 85 (`isSilent` flag drives `<Text dimColor>(No output)</Text>`). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:101-119 (walks BashOutputView's children, asserts one of them carries `(No output)` text).

edgeCases.edgeCase: Bash result with non-zero exit code is visually distinct from zero exit code in the rendered output
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:71-76, 86-90 (`isFailure` controls `color="red"` vs `dimColor`). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:121-163 (asserts color="red" + falsy dimColor for exit_code=1, and undefined color + dimColor=true for exit_code=0).

edgeCases.edgeCase: Bash result with stderr but empty stdout still shows the stderr block
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:84 (`{stderr.length > 0 ? <Text color="red">{stderr}</Text> : null}` independent of stdout). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:189-215 (Round 3 isolated test: feeds `<bash-stdout></bash-stdout><bash-stderr>oops</bash-stderr>[exit_code=1]`, finds a child with `props.color === 'red'` and `props.children === 'oops'`, AND asserts no `(No output)` indicator appears). Closes the high-severity gap from Round 2.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Bash result with stdout containing ANSI escape sequences renders without re-interpreting them as terminal control
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:83 (raw `<Text>{stdout}</Text>` with no preprocessing). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:217-233 (`\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m text` survives verbatim into a Text child's `props.children`).

edgeCases.edgeCase: Bash result whose stdout exceeds the runtime maxResultSizeChars is truncated rather than crashing the renderer
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:44-51, 62-63 (`BASH_OUTPUT_TRUNCATION_LIMIT = 8 * 1024`, `truncateForDisplay` clips and appends `[N chars truncated]`). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:165-187 (50,000-char stdout, asserts rendered length < 10,000 and contains `chars truncated`).

edgeCases.edgeCase: renderToolResultMessage receives the legacy single-string content (from a tool that did not go through formatStructuredToolResult) and falls back to the generic Text renderer instead of throwing
edgeCases.evidence: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/bridges/tool-stubs.tsx:184-198 (generic fallback when joinedContent has no `<bash-stdout>` envelope). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:33-41 (`createBridgeTool('Bash').renderToolResultMessage('raw legacy string with no envelope', ...)` returns an element whose type is NOT BashOutputView).

issues.message: The vi.mock pattern at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:10-18 substitutes Box and Text with functions that return null. This is enough to keep React.createElement working and to inspect element.type and element.props, but it does not exercise ink's rendering pipeline. The tests therefore prove createElement was called with the correct component and props but cannot prove ink itself would not crash on the produced tree (e.g. invalid prop types, unsupported children shapes). This is acceptable for parity-of-shape coverage but should be stated in the matrix as an explicit limitation, or a follow-up integration test using ink-testing-library's `render()` should be scheduled. Not blocking on its own — the underlying upstream-component-flip note is already on tool-stubs.tsx:36-42 and the wire-shape lock test at lines 236-269 protects the contract that an eventual flip to the literal upstream component requires no shape changes.

The implementer's bypass for the upstream UserBashOutputMessage import chain (tool-stubs.tsx:9-17, 31-42 and the comment on the test file lines 3-9) explains the bun:bundle / require('../memdir/teamMemPaths.js') feature gate that vitest cannot resolve. This explanation lives only in inline comments. Capturing it once in the matrix's `notes` field, in parity/tool-result-renderer-dispatch.json under this row, or in a sibling docs file would prevent a future implementer from re-attempting the literal-upstream-component path without the context. Not blocking — the row's behavior is correct and the bypass is fully reversible once the dist resolution path is in scope.

Top-level `testFiles` at parity/tool-result-renderer-dispatch.json:18-21 lists only `runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.test.tsx` and `runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.test.ts`, while the per-row `tests` array at line 41-45 correctly lists three files including `runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx` and `runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts`. Additionally, `runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.test.tsx` does not exist on disk — the file referenced at the top level appears to be stale. The per-row tests array is the authoritative list and matches reality (all three files exist and pass), so this does not block the row, but the top-level `testFiles` entry should be reconciled (either remove the non-existent `tool-stubs.test.tsx` or add the two `runtime/src/tools/*.test.*` files) before the contract is closed out for the other two rows.

testsExecuted: cd /home/tetsuo/git/agenc-core-tool-result-dispatch/runtime && node_modules/.bin/vitest run --root . src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.test.ts  # exit 0, 37 passed (15 + 13 + 9), all three declared row tests pass; row's declared command runs cleanly. The new isolated stderr-only test at tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:189-215 is among the 13 passing in that file.
